headline: Meta ties up with Ambani's Reliance for AI data center in India
summary: Facebook-parent Meta said on Wednesday that it has tied up ‌with Indian billionaire Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries ‌for the U.S. company's first AI-enabled data center in ​India. Reliance will build a data center with 168 MW capacity in Jamnagar in the western Indian state of Gujarat, which Meta will ‌lease, with options ⁠to scale, according to the statement. Meta said India's massive user base and ⁠fast-growing digital economy make it an ideal place to invest.

Reliance will build a data center with 168 MW capacity in Jamnagar in the western Indian state of Gujarat, which Meta will ‌lease, with options ⁠to scale, according to the statement. Meta said India's massive user base and ⁠fast-growing digital economy make it an ideal place to invest.

headline: Global AI debt issuance to top $500 billion in 2026, Morgan Stanley says
summary: Morgan Stanley forecasts AI-related global debt issuance to more than double ‌to nearly $570 billion in 2026, pointing to ‌rising bond supply and credit market activity as hyperscalers turn ​to alternative funding sources to meet massive AI-driven capex needs. Here are some details: • Tech companies that have long relied on strong cash flows are ‌increasingly turning to ⁠debt financing as investment needs surge • Morgan Stanley estimates AI-related global debt issuance ⁠stood at nearly $236 billion as of May 31, 2026, fourfold more than the same period last ​year • Hyperscalers ​Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft and ​Meta are expected to ‌spend $700 billion in outlays this year • Morgan Stanley ex...

headline: LinkedIn launches new effort in push to capture business advertisers
summary: LinkedIn is aiming to burnish its position as a go-to platform for business advertisers with a new team of marketing experts ‌that it expects to generate an annualized run rate of $100 million next fiscal year, ‌a source familiar with the matter said.  The Microsoft-owned platform on Wednesday unveiled BrandWorks, the newly assembled team that aims ​to deliver higher-performing ad campaigns for customers ranging from software giant SAP to privately held website hosting platform Webflow.  "We're developing services that are designed to meet the marketer where they are," said Alex Josephson, VP of BrandWorks, who previously built a similar offering called Twitter Next.

content.title: LinkedIn launches new effort in push to capture business advertisers
content.summary: LinkedIn is aiming to burnish its position as a go-to platform for business advertisers with a new team of marketing experts ‌that it expects to generate an annualized run rate of $100 million next fiscal year, ‌a source familiar with the matter said.  The Microsoft-owned platform on Wednesday unveiled BrandWorks, the newly assembled team that aims ​to deliver higher-performing ad campaigns for customers ranging from software giant SAP to privately held website hosting platform Webflow.  "We're developing services that are designed to meet the marketer where they are," said Alex Josephson, VP of BrandWorks, who previously built a similar offering called Twitter Next.

headline: Meta deepens partnership with Ambani's Reliance with AI data centre
summary: Meta Platforms will lease an AI-ready data centre to be built by billionaire Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries, ‌the U.S. tech major said on Wednesday, deepening ties with the Indian ‌conglomerate.  The deal comes at a time when India has seen a surge in hyperscale data centre ​builds led by U.S. cloud majors such as Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google, as New Delhi seeks to draw in investment in AI.  In February, Reliance committed about $110 billion and Adani outlined $100 billion in investments to position India as an AI ‌hub.

content.title: Meta deepens partnership with Ambani's Reliance with AI data centre
content.summary: Meta Platforms will lease an AI-ready data centre to be built by billionaire Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries, ‌the U.S. tech major said on Wednesday, deepening ties with the Indian ‌conglomerate.  The deal comes at a time when India has seen a surge in hyperscale data centre ​builds led by U.S. cloud majors such as Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google, as New Delhi seeks to draw in investment in AI.  In February, Reliance committed about $110 billion and Adani outlined $100 billion in investments to position India as an AI ‌hub.

headline: Trump says he thinks AI companies will agree to 'giving back' to the public
summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that he expects ‌top artificial intelligence companies to agree to "giving back" to the ‌public, an apparent reference to a possible government stake in the firms.  "I'm going to ​have meetings with the top 12 or 15 executives very shortly, and we're talking about giving back something to the public, and if we do that, the public will become very rich," Trump told reporters in the ‌White House’s Oval Office.  A Trump spokesperson did not immediately respond to a request for more details about the president meeting with AI executives.

content.title: Trump says he thinks AI companies will agree to 'giving back' to the public
content.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that he expects ‌top artificial intelligence companies to agree to "giving back" to the ‌public, an apparent reference to a possible government stake in the firms. A Trump spokesperson did not immediately respond to a request for more details about the president meeting with AI executives.

headline: Google and Meta denied new trial in youth social media addiction case
summary: A California state court judge has denied motions by Meta Platforms and Google's YouTube seeking ‌a new trial after a jury found the companies liable for ‌designing social media platforms that are harmful to young people.  Los Angeles Superior Court Judge ​Carolyn Kuhl ruled on the motions on Tuesday, according to court documents.  The companies had sought a new trial in a lawsuit filed by a woman who said she became addicted to Google's YouTube and Meta's Instagram ‌at a young age ⁠because of their attention-grabbing design.

content.title: Google and Meta denied new trial in youth social media addiction case
content.summary: A California state court judge has denied motions by Meta Platforms and Google's YouTube seeking ‌a new trial after a jury found the companies liable for ‌designing social media platforms that are harmful to young people.  Los Angeles Superior Court Judge ​Carolyn Kuhl ruled on the motions on Tuesday, according to court documents.  The companies had sought a new trial in a lawsuit filed by a woman who said she became addicted to Google's YouTube and Meta's Instagram ‌at a young age ⁠because of their attention-grabbing design.

headline: Jeremy Strong becomes Mark Zuckerberg in 'Social Network' sequel
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Ines Ferré and Brooke DiPalma come on Market Domination to eye some of Wednesday's top headlines, including movie lovers' first look at Jeremy Strong's — best known for HBO's corporate drama "Succession" — portrayal of Meta Platforms (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg in "The Social Reckoning." The film is a direct sequel to Aaron Sorkin's "The Social Network."

The film is a direct sequel to Aaron Sorkin's "The Social Network."

headline: AI stocks: MANGOS ascends, replacing FAANG position

MANGOS consists of Meta Platforms (META), Anthropic (ANTH.PVT), Nvidia (NVDA), Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), OpenAI (OPAI.PVT), and SpaceX (SPCX, SPAX.PVT). headline: Drive Social Media Offers an Update to Clients Regarding the 2026 Meta Ads Attribution Window Changes
summary: ST LOUIS, June 10, 2026--Earlier this year, Meta made sweeping API changes that completely altered attribution tracking and targeting for advertisers. The result was that many marketing and ad companies and their clients witnessed their Meta Ad conversion numbers drop by anywhere from 20% to 40% seemingly overnight. However, Drive Social Media is assuring its customers that while Meta’s recent changes affect how ads are measured, they do not affect the actual performance or ROI of ads.

However, Drive Social Media is assuring its customers that while Meta’s recent changes affect how ads are measured, they do not affect the actual performance or ROI of ads.

headline: Meta And Reliance Partner On India AI Hub To Support Meta’s Growth
summary: Meta Platforms (NasdaqGS:META) has agreed to lease India's largest AI-enabled data center from Reliance Industries in Gujarat. The facility is described as built to suit for Meta, with renewable power and advanced cooling technology. This is Meta's first dedicated data center of this type in India, in a sector the government classifies as strategic national infrastructure. For you as an investor, this move ties directly into how Meta earns from advertising, social platforms and its AI...

headline: QQQ vs. VGT: The Best Way to Own Big Tech?
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) and the Vanguard Information Technology ETF (NYSEARCA:VGT) are often shoved into the same “own Big Tech” bucket, but they answer different questions. QQQ tracks the Nasdaq-100, a listing-based index that is growth-heavy. VGT tracks the MSCI US Investable Market Information Technology 25/50 Index, a pure GICS sector fund. That distinction ... headline: There’s a New Stock to Play the AI Power Boom, and It’s Down Today
summary: Newly-listed power company ERock could appeal to investors looking for another way to play the AI boom.  Erock started trading on the New York Stock Exchange around midday on Wednesday, slipping 5% in early action.  ERock makes natural gas-powered engines and generators that can be placed on-site at data centers or other large businesses to provide electricity.
